description: Currently the blinking/animating cursor inside the search input only animates while the input is focused/active. User wants the cursor animation to play CONTINUOUSLY — even when the user hasn't clicked into the search bar — to draw attention to the search affordance. Applies to BOTH: (1) home page search bar, (2) publications page search bar. Scope: locate the search input components and the cursor animation logic (likely a pseudo-element or a wrapper span with a CSS animation gated on :focus or a focused state). Remove the focus-gating so the animation runs unconditionally. Verify the animation doesn't conflict with the real text caret when the user actually types. Acceptance: cursor animation is visible on page load on both home and publications pages without the user interacting; still behaves correctly once the user starts typing.
title: Search bar cursor animation: keep animating even when not focused (home + publications)
